source_paper_title: Cardinal-Utility Matching Markets: The Quest for Envy-Freeness, Pareto-Optimality, and Efficient Computability
context:
Section 4 (Conclusion), page 23 (PDF pagination; parsed text page 23/28)

Is Nash bargaining the best we can do for one-sided markets or is there a way to compute an \(\alpha\)-envy-free and Pareto-optimal lottery for \(\alpha < 2\) in polynomial time?
title: Polynomial-time computation of an \(\alpha\)-envy-free and Pareto-optimal lottery with \(\alpha<2\) in one-sided cardinal-utility matching markets
background_latex:
Consider a one-sided cardinal-utility matching market with a set of agents \(A\) and a set of goods \(G\), where \(|A|=|G|=n\). Each agent \(i\in A\) has a nonnegative utility \(u_{ij}\in\mathbb{Q}_{\ge 0}\) for each good \(j\in G\). An (ex-ante) lottery over integral perfect matchings can be represented by a fractional perfect matching \(x=(x_{ij})_{i\in A,j\in G}\) satisfying \(\sum_{j\in G} x_{ij}=1\) for all \(i\in A\), \(\sum_{i\in A} x_{ij}=1\) for all \(j\in G\), and \(x_{ij}\ge 0\). Agent \(i\)'s expected utility under \(x\) is \(U_i(x)=\sum_{j\in G} u_{ij}x_{ij}\).

For \(\alpha\ge 1\), an allocation \(x\) is \(\alpha\)-approximately envy-free (\(\alpha\)-EF) if for all agents \(i,i'\in A\), \(U_i(x)\ge \frac{1}{\alpha}U_i(x_{i'})\), where \(U_i(x_{i'})=\sum_{j\in G}u_{ij}x_{i'j}\) is the utility agent \(i\) would obtain from agent \(i'\)'s (random) bundle. An allocation \(x\) is Pareto-optimal (PO) if there is no other fractional perfect matching \(y\) such that \(U_i(y)\ge U_i(x)\) for all \(i\in A\) and \(U_{i^*}(y)>U_{i^*}(x)\) for some agent \(i^*\in A\).
problem_definition_latex: Design a polynomial-time algorithm that, given \((A,G,(u_{ij})_{i\in A,j\in G})\), computes a fractional perfect matching \(x\) that is Pareto-optimal and \(\alpha\)-approximately envy-free for some universal constant \(\alpha<2\); or prove that no such polynomial-time algorithm exists under a standard complexity assumption.
latex:
Consider a one-sided cardinal-utility matching market with a set of agents \(A\) and a set of goods \(G\), where \(|A|=|G|=n\). Each agent \(i\in A\) has a nonnegative utility \(u_{ij}\in\mathbb{Q}_{\ge 0}\) for each good \(j\in G\). An (ex-ante) lottery over integral perfect matchings can be represented by a fractional perfect matching \(x=(x_{ij})_{i\in A,j\in G}\) satisfying \(\sum_{j\in G} x_{ij}=1\) for all \(i\in A\), \(\sum_{i\in A} x_{ij}=1\) for all \(j\in G\), and \(x_{ij}\ge 0\). Agent \(i\)'s expected utility under \(x\) is \(U_i(x)=\sum_{j\in G} u_{ij}x_{ij}\).

For \(\alpha\ge 1\), an allocation \(x\) is \(\alpha\)-approximately envy-free (\(\alpha\)-EF) if for all agents \(i,i'\in A\), \(U_i(x)\ge \frac{1}{\alpha}U_i(x_{i'})\), where \(U_i(x_{i'})=\sum_{j\in G}u_{ij}x_{i'j}\) is the utility agent \(i\) would obtain from agent \(i'\)'s (random) bundle. An allocation \(x\) is Pareto-optimal (PO) if there is no other fractional perfect matching \(y\) such that \(U_i(y)\ge U_i(x)\) for all \(i\in A\) and \(U_{i^*}(y)>U_{i^*}(x)\) for some agent \(i^*\in A\).

Design a polynomial-time algorithm that, given \((A,G,(u_{ij})_{i\in A,j\in G})\), computes a fractional perfect matching \(x\) that is Pareto-optimal and \(\alpha\)-approximately envy-free for some universal constant \(\alpha<2\); or prove that no such polynomial-time algorithm exists under a standard complexity assumption.
